context: Tests whether the agent implements revenue variance decomposition using the volume-effect/price-effect methodology, with correct filtering thresholds, SQL safety patterns, and result ordering.

checklist.name: Volume effect formula
checklist.description: Volume effect is calculated as (current_units - prior_units) × prior_ASP — not current ASP or average ASP

checklist.name: Price effect formula
checklist.description: Price effect is calculated as prior_units × (current_ASP - prior_ASP) — not current units

checklist.name: Variance minimum filter
checklist.description: Rows with total_variance absolute value below $1,000 are excluded from the output

checklist.name: Effect minimum threshold
checklist.description: Volume and price effects are only reported in explanations when their absolute value exceeds $500

checklist.name: NULLIF for division
checklist.description: Any SQL expressions dividing revenue by unit counts use NULLIF to avoid division-by-zero errors

checklist.name: Order by absolute variance
checklist.description: Results are ordered by absolute variance descending (largest movers first)

checklist.name: Result count limit
checklist.description: Output is limited to at most 20 channel/category combinations

checklist.name: Channel and category breakdown
checklist.description: Variance is broken down by both channel and category dimensions (not just one dimension)

checklist.name: Both absolute and pct shown
checklist.description: Output or explanation includes both absolute dollar variance and percentage variance — not just one

checklist.name: Human-readable explanation
checklist.description: Output includes a plain-language explanation string per row (e.g. 'DTC / Apparel decreased by $12,400. Drivers: 230 lower units...')

checklist.name: COALESCE for new segments
checklist.description: Query handles new channel/category combinations (present in current period but not prior) using COALESCE or LEFT JOIN to avoid null comparisons
